methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Charm Industrial is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Peter Reinhardt and Shaun Meehan that has raised $100M+. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 comparable companies—1 public and 8 private—operating in the same sector and stage. Key private comparables include Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, CarbonCapture Inc., and Cemvita, with valuations ranging from $120M to $500M.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $100M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage expansion, yielding $190M. The peer-median method derives a $310M valuation from the five-company median across same-sector, same-stage peers, with a range spanning $120M to $500M. These methods produce a weighted triangulated range of $162M to $356M, with a median implied value of $250M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $250M±$100M, with the median estimate at $250M. The primary uncertainties center on whether Charm's operating metrics and market position align with the higher-end comparables (Pano AI, Sage Geosystems) or the lower-end outliers (Cemvita). Capital efficiency, technology maturity, and revenue traction relative to peers would narrow this range but are not available in current data.
